name: Queer Quiet Hours - Meetup
description:
Queer Quiet Hours returns for July 2026!
Register here - http://bit.ly/adhdqueeple
 
A soft little corner for LGBTQIA+ folks to slow down, make art, read, journal, daydream, or simply exist in shared company.
 
There’s no pressure to interact. Come with your own project, or just arrive as you are. We’ll also have art materials around for anyone who wants to use them.
 
✨ This space is for LGBTQIA+ folks only. We haven’t opened it up to allies yet. Thank you for understanding.
 
🆓 Free to join, but spots are limited. Link in bio.
 
📅  12 JULY, 2026
⏰ Sundays, 2 - 5 PM
📍 @attagalatta, Indiranagar, Bangalore
📸 No photos taken without clear consent.

name: Emotional Well- Being for Women - Workshop
description:
Our life journeys are different from the time we are born, so the definition of what emotional well-being means to each one of us ought to be unique to us. There is no one-size-fits-all approach.
In an age where everything we buy is personalised, do you want to know what your emotional well-being looks like? Then, this session is for you. A reflective session curated to help you discover the building blocks of your emotional well-being, your way.
Facilitated by Sandya Jadhav, an Emotional Well-being Life Coach (ICF ACC Practitioner). She empowers women to build emotionally healthy lives that resonate with their true selves.
Age Group: 25 +
Website: www.sathyastudio.com

name: Get in touch with your Body - Body Awareness Workshop
description:
“When was the last time you played without worrying about being good at it?”
As children, play came naturally. We explored, experimented, imagined, and expressed ourselves freely. Somewhere along the way, many of us traded play for productivity, performance, and perfection.
Get In Touch With Your Body is a playful movement and expressive arts workshop that invites you to return to that space of curiosity. Through creative movement, simple art-based activities, and shared experiences, you'll explore what happens when you let go of "doing it right" and allow yourself to simply engage, create, and move.
Play is more than fun. It helps us become more flexible, creative, connected, and present. It opens the door to self-expression, reduces stress, nurtures resilience, and reminds us that growth doesn't always have to be serious.
No dance experience. No art experience. No pressure to perform.
Just bring your curiosity, your willingness to explore, and a little room for play.
Come move, create, connect, and rediscover the joy of being fully alive in your body.
Message or Call 9880191630 to register

name: Playback Theatre Jam - Interactive theater workshop
description:
How would it be to indulge in an evening of fun, spontaneity, and creativity while getting a taster of a very powerful format of theatre - Playback Theatre.
What is Playback Theatre?
A socio-improv, interactive format of theatre where audience members share real-life moments and feelings, and actors improvise to reflect back the stories, on-the-spot.
What would the evening have?
 A friendly, open, non-judgmental space
 Opportunity to connect with a creative community
 A nudge to think out-of-the-box
The jam is open to everyone - seasoned Playbackers, beginners, people who have never indulged in theatre; basically, anyone curious to experience this unique theatre form and have fun being creative.
How would it be to indulge in an evening of fun, spontaneity, and creativity while getting a taster of a very powerful format of theatre - Playback Theatre.

name: Auto Akka and the Hot Seat - Book reading and workshop
description:
Bangalore is sizzling! And young Ravi’s favourite blue, sticker-covered water bottle is officially missing. Jump into the hot seat with Auto Akka, the city's coolest auto rickshaw driver, for an interactive story reading and creative workshop! Based on the children's storybook Auto Akka and the Hot Seat, this fun reading session will take us all on a fast-paced hunt through bustling streets while exploring the real-world realities of urban heat and climate resilience. After the book reading, children will get hands-on and creative, sketching, designing, and inventing their very own climate resilient, cool autos to beat the heat!
About the Facilitators: 
Ujjvala Krishna is a climate and urban researcher drawn to the intersections of people, policy, and the built environment. Believing that cities are simultaneously wonderful and disastrously ad-hoc, her work explores the friction and negotiations between the various systems that shape our urban spaces. As an architect by training, she is perpetually asking, "how did this really get built?" - seeking to bridge the gap between the chaotic, lived reality of the city, and what shapes it.
Radha Patkar is a visual communication designer from Mumbai, currently based in Bangalore. She works across illustration, comics, and visual storytelling, creating stories inspired by everyday life. Her work draws from the people, places, and small moments that often go unnoticed, turning them into visual narratives that feel familiar, observant, and human.
Nithya Gowda is an urban planner and community engagement specialist. She has worked at the intersection of mobility, climate resilience, urban ecology, and grassroots engagement. Her work consistently turns community knowledge into planning evidence: from leading walkability audits across 300 km of Bengaluru's footpaths to co-authoring a nationally replicable safe routes to school guidebook. Fluent in Kannada, she engages directly with communities that planning processes most often leave out.

name: The Pranic Breakthrough: Heal - Heal. Transform. Break Free
description:
The Pranic Breakthrough: Heal. Transform. Break Free. Join us at Atta Galatta on July 17th Friday 6-7PM IST to explore how Pranic Healing helps you easily break free from endless stuckness & struggles.

Get to know how simply and properly you can,
- Release stress, pain, limiting habits, fears, blocks and negative thought patterns.
- Heal your mind, empower your actions, reconnect with your inner wisdom and reclaim your inner power.
- Transform into the highest version of yourself, and step into the greatest possibilities of your life.
- Questions & Answers.

name: ALT EFF Film Club x Atta Galatta - Short films we love, followed by an impromptu open mic on the environment.
description:
We are in penultimate screening of the ALT EFF Film Club, where we have been able to bring together people who care deeply about cinema and the environment, and who are seeking spaces to connect with like-minded individuals in Bangalore.
For our July edition, we present a curated short-film programme on 17th  July 7 PM, bringing together three films that explore environmental storytelling through distinct and compelling lenses.
Line-up: 
Would You Still Love Me If I was a Sticky Frog?
Papillon
Shera
Following the screening, ALT EFF Voices invites audience members into an open mic format to share brief, personal responses to environmental prompts, creating a space for reflection, conversation, and exchange.

name: Humzubaan - Hindi and Urdu Poetry Meet
description:
Humzubaan, conceived by Siddharth Surana is an effort to bring together Hindi/Urdu literature enthusiasts together to celebrate the beauty of poetry. The next meet up is happening on Friday, 17th July, 7 PM at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar.
Recite your own or your favourite author’s poems or simply soak in the magic of poetry. Click Here to register as audience or performer. Performance Submissions are open till 12th July.
We will inform you if your poem is selected for the meet. Even if your poem is not selected, you’re most welcome to attend as an audience.
Please note:
1. The poems can be your own original work or of another author. You need to mention clearly and send in your entry in the above-mentioned form.
2. Poems/songs should be in Hindi/Urdu only.
3. If you’re planning to perform with music/props, please mention in the form. You would need to arrange for the instruments and set-up at the venue.
About the host:
Siddharth is founder of AgriCentral, a digital platform serving 11 million farmers. He writes in Hindi, Urdu and English-mostly poems, short stories and essays but with long breaks. He is also an avid quizzer, trekker and a national-level gold medallist archer..

name: Round Table of Stories: The Revival Edit - Storytelling  and Creative Visualisation
description:
The Liminal Space brings to you The Round Table of Stories: The Revival Edit. 
Join in for an afternoon of sharing through storytelling and Creative Visualisation Tools. Ritu and Anupa are highly experienced Storytellers and Facilitators who are Mental health practitioners as well. They come with 25+ years of experience in the related field, and they will take you on a journey of deep self-discovery to help you Create, Learn, and Grow.
Message 9900021372 to register

name: Unhurried Afternoon - Sip and Paint Experience
description: An Unhurried Afternoon is a beginner-friendly sip-and-paint experience designed for those looking to slow down and spend a creative afternoon away from the rush of everyday life. Over two relaxed hours, participants will create a cozy café-inspired acrylic painting featuring a sunflower and blue coffee mug while enjoying a beverage and good company. No prior painting experience is required. All materials are provided, and participants will be guided step-by-step through the process. Ticket includes painting materials and one beverage

name: BWW Short Story Community - short story community
description:
The BWW Short Fiction Community at Atta Galatta will discuss
The Readers (Short story attached)
By Ben Lerner
Quietly cerebral and emotionally precise, The Readers follows moments of modern distance, memory, and fragile human connection against the backdrop of a sudden digital silence. Through intimate reflections and finely detailed observations after an unexpected accident cuts the narrator off from technology, the story explores themes of isolation, the instability of recollection, and the subtle ways people attempt to understand one another while carrying their own private worlds. Ben Lerner’s restrained yet evocative prose captures the ache of communication and the beauty hidden within ordinary, low-tech moments. 
Read the text and come to the event.

name: Taishogoto to Bulbul Tarang - Informative Session and Musical Perfomance
description: An informative session for young minds on the Genesis and Evolution of the Bulbul Tarang, featuring a live demonstration and exhibition and a concert.  Experience the sounds, stories, and evolution of Bulbul Tarang  bridging the past and present.  Join us for an unforgettable evening celebrating the timeless charm and global journey of this remarkable instrument.

name: Divine Delicacies - Storytelling and Menu Tasting Session
description:
Divine Delicacies offers a sensory journey through India's sacred kitchens. Join storyteller Aparna Jaishankar and Bharatnatyam dancer and educator Rajeshwari Aravind to uncover the myths, history, and folklore behind beloved temple prasadams through a very unique source- their architecture! 
As Aparna shares the deep-rooted myths, legends, and folklores of ancient temples, the audience will taste authentic prasadams uniquely tied to each temple’s deity. Rajeshwari will unpack the silent language of these divine spaces by showcasing images of the frozen narratives of gopurams, pillars, and carvings, the sacred geometry and designs that turned stones into a spiritual canvas, and decode a few of the cultural motifs in these visual stories.
Experience Temple architecture brought to life through food and stories.
About Rajeshwari:
Bharatanatyam has shaped Rajeshwari Aravind’s world for as long as she can remember. What began as a childhood initiation grew into an enduring pursuit marked by constant exploration and refinement. Rajeshwari works for Aalaap, an Arts Consulting and Management company based in Chennai. She is also involved in organising, archiving and documenting a wide range of artistic projects in the field of dance.

name: A Graphic Novelist's Journey - Author Discussion
description:
Step into the extraordinary world of graphic storytelling with one of India's most acclaimed graphic novelists!
Join us for "A Graphic Novelist's Journey", an engaging conversation with Appupen, where we'll explore his creative process, visual narratives, and the power of graphic novels.
✨ Special Discussion: Vaadivaasal with BooksWithPammy
DM to Register: 8317454468

name: Magnet and Keychain Making Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join me for a creative workshop of magnet and keychain making using acrylic paints.
This is a beginner-friendly session where you can explore your artistic talent and express them in the form of unique and vibrant keychains and magnets that capture your vivid imagination. Personalised art pieces to take home that would be an expression of your inner self!
What’s provided
All the materials will be provided, along with expert guidance
What you need to bring
Just bring your creative vibes along

name: Meet the Author: Indrani Talukdar - Book Discussion
description:
What happens when love becomes a matter of life and death?
Join award-winning author Indrani Talukdar in conversation with Reena Pereira as they discuss Till the Dust Settles, a compelling collection of fiction that moves between danger, humour, and the complexities of everyday life in India.
At the heart of the book is a gripping novelette about a couple forced to flee their rural community after an illicit romance puts them in mortal danger. Alongside it are witty and insightful stories of small-town India—featuring eccentric dowry negotiations, family dramas, unexpected acts of courage, and moments of quiet transformation.
Drawing from her experiences across India and abroad, Talukdar's writing explores the tensions between tradition and change, individual desire and social expectation, while capturing the humour, contradictions, and resilience of ordinary lives.
Whether you're a lover of fiction, curious about contemporary Indian storytelling, or simply looking for an engaging literary evening, this is an opportunity to meet the author, hear about her writing journey, and participate in a lively discussion followed by audience Q&A.
Author: Indrani Talukdar
Moderator: Reena Pereira
Date: Sunday, 19 July 2026
Time: 5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Venue: Atta Galatta, 178, 5th Main, 9th Cross, First Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Borders are more than lines on a map; they are stories of power, ambition, and the lives they reshape. This month, we journey through the forgotten history of The Great Hedge of India, uncovering an extraordinary colonial experiment that sought to control trade and people through an immense living barrier. What does it mean to rediscover the hidden landscapes of history and the legacies they leave behind?
 
Book of the month: The Great Hedge of India by Roy Moxham
When: Saturday, 25th July, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
 
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots.
 
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat.

name: Let Poetry Be - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This monthly gathering will see poetry coming to life, through reading, music, art and any other forms of artistic expression, at Atta Galatta. Listeners and performers are all welcome!
Date: 25th July
Time: 4pm
Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
Event specifics & submission guidelines –
1. Theme - “Things in my drawer”(an item you are emotionally attached to!)
2. Works can be original or of other poets, performed live
3. All languages welcome
4. Participants can read to music (acoustic instrument/s), dramatise, use props & costumes
5. Each participant will enjoy 5 minutes of reading time
6. Kindly submit your poem and name to letpoetrybe@gmail.com on or before 23rd July midnight. WE WILL NOT ACCEPT ATTACHMENTS OR LINKS. SUBJECT OF YOUR MAIL MUST READ AS 'Entry for-July LPB meet-up'
7. You may also send entries that are not related to the theme
8. The event will begin at 4pm sharp and will be held at Atta Galatta. Participants to report not later than 3:45 pm to hear about performance order and meet the host
9. For any further clarifications, write to us at letpoetrybe@gmail.com
You are now welcome to flood our inbox!

name: This Isn't S*xy -
description:
Some ambitions are loud.
Others quietly follow you around for years.
The business.
The novel.
The healthier body.
The career change.
The city you want to move to.
The person you suspect you're capable of becoming.
And sometimes...
You don't even know what that thing is yet.
You just know your current life doesn't feel fully yours.
This Isn't S*xy isn't about motivation.
It's about sitting down for two uninterrupted hours to honestly ask yourself what you want—and building a system that makes it much harder to ignore once you know.
We'll map your priorities, challenge vague ideas, translate them into concrete actions, and leave with a personalized operating system for the months ahead.
No productivity gurus.
No life hacks.
No vision boards.
Just thoughtful conversations, paper, pencils, and a room full of people trying to build lives they're excited to wake up to.

name: Divya Leelamritham - Divine stories through Bharatanatyam
description:
Experience an enchanting evening of Bharatanatyam as timeless stories of Ganesha, Rama, Krishna, and Devi come alive through dance, music, and expression. A celebration of devotion, grace, and the joy of surrendering to the Divine, Divya Leelamritham invites audiences of all ages to immerse themselves in the beauty of India's rich cultural heritage.
Join us for an evening filled with devotion, storytelling, rhythm, and the timeless beauty of Bharatanatyam.

name: Curious Minds Trivia Night  - Trivia Night
description:
Forget everything you think a trivia night is. 
Curious Minds Trivia Night is an interactive, people-first experience that blends pop culture, teamwork, strategy, and friendly competition into one memorable evening. Before the event, participants fill out a short registration form sharing their favourite movies, TV shows, music, and interests. Parts of the trivia are then built around those responses, making every edition unique and tailored to the people in the room. Expect a mix of visual rounds, quote challenges, picture rounds, strategic betting rounds, and a few surprises along the way. Some rounds will test your memory, others your observation, and a few will reward teamwork, creativity, and quick thinking.
Whether you’re a seasoned trivia enthusiast or someone looking for a genuinely good evening with strangers — this event is designed to bring people together through laughter, conversation, and a little healthy rivalry.
Come with a team or walk in solo — we’ll find you one. All you need is curiosity and a willingness to play.
Come once. Come back forever

name: TechnoCats - Film Screening
description:
A search for purpose in the fiercely competitive world of the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT).
SYNOPSIS 
Seven young people from seven parts of the country (the Seven Samurai) dream of joining the IIT. Competing against one million applicants, they gain admission, but are soon disillusioned. Will they make their presence felt on the world stage? Or burn out along the way? The filmmaker, an IIT alumnus, follows the Seven Samurai over seven years—from their first semester to the third year of their careers. Interwoven with their journey are four of the filmmaker’s classmates from 35 years ago, including one who made an audiovisual on IIT student life. As he rediscovers old slides and struggles to sync them with the audio track, this forgotten past frames the film’s exploration of winning at all cost. 
DIRECTOR’S STATEMENT 
I enrolled for a B.Tech. in electrical engineering at IIT Delhi, but was dejected at the barrage of tests and emphasis on grades over all else. Where was the time to learn, ponder, speculate? 35 years later, I was surprised to meet students from very different backgrounds who felt similarly. I decided to revisit my past and connect with some of my classmates. Their stories are interwoven with  those of the seven students at the heart of this film, to create a conversation across generations.
